title: iberis gypsicola
intro: Iberis gypsicola, also known as rock candytuft, is a flowering plant species in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and succulent foliage. Traditionally, Iberis gypsicola has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate coughs and colds, and soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Iberis gypsicola is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its roots and leaves can be dried and used in herbal remedies.
